sources.title: Pre-commit review — M0005 WebDev tab + IZeroBrowser bridge (1 Should-fix applied inline)
Mission M0005 — Settings WebDev tab + IZeroBrowser native bridge
Build doctor — release gate v0.3.1 (PASS, 0 regressions, 6/6 validations clean)
Release pipeline gate — v0.3.1 (both security-guard + build-doctor PASS)

tried: Released v0.3.1 through the release-build-pipeline gate (security-guard → build-doctor) — small 9-commit delta dominated by XAML styling, harness docs, and JS viewer code
Shipped mission M0005 — WebDev tab in Settings hosting an in-app WebView2 browser that loads local web apps from Wasm/ via SetVirtualHostNameToFolderMapping
Wired IZeroBrowser as a native bridge contract: JS calls .NET through chrome.webview.postMessage JSON RPC ({id, op, args} → {id, ok, result, error}) — no HTTP, no network
Placed IZeroBrowser in ZeroCommon (WPF-free) so future .wasm plugins can compile against the contract without dragging in PresentationFramework
Reused VoiceRuntimeFactory + VoicePlaybackService for the first sandbox app (voice-test) instead of cloning the TTS pipeline — single source of truth with Settings/Voice
Auto-invoked code-coach Mode 2 on the M0005 staged diff per the pre-commit-review engine — 1 Should-fix (missing webDevView.Dispose() in OnUnloaded) surfaced and applied inline before commit
solved: Sandbox-with-bridge pattern proven end-to-end: WebView2 virtual host mapping + postMessage round-trip works; the voice-test web UI calls SpeakAsync from one line of JS and hears native TTS play back
v0.3.1 release gate cleared with zero Critical/High findings; native DLL pinning, version pipeline, EF migrations, and -cli detection all verified unchanged

Pre-commit review → inline-fix loop closes the gap that 'Should-fix becomes deferred work' — applying the fix before commit means no GitHub issue handoff is needed and the audit log still shows the finding
remaining: M0005 next stage: host→JS event channel for streaming (mic capture + partial transcripts) — bridge has the slot but no producer wired yet
Full STT → LLM → TTS loop inside the sandbox is still a WPF-hosted feature; sandbox parity is at 'Now' stage of the roadmap, not 'Plugin'

v0.3.0 carry-forward security items still open across three consecutive releases — BuildTerminalSummaryPrompt fence-injection (Low / OWASP LLM01), cloud TTS per-bubble warning (Info), voice transcript shell-escape (Medium)
Real .wasm modules not yet loadable; folder structure under Wasm/ is prepared but the plugin SDK contract beyond IZeroBrowser is undefined

learned.lead: Putting the bridge contract in the WPF-free common layer is the small upfront cost that unlocks every future portability move — the seam pattern that makes the rest of the architecture worth doing.
learned.body: M0005 could have shipped with IZeroBrowser sitting next to its WPF implementation in AgentZeroWpf and still functioned today. Pushing the interface down into ZeroCommon adds exactly one project boundary and zero runtime cost — but it means future .wasm plugins (compiled C# → WASI, or Rust via wasmtime), a hypothetical CefSharp-host swap, and unit tests of the bridge dispatcher can all target the contract without dragging in PresentationFramework. This is the same lesson the actor codebase already absorbed with ITerminalSession: keep logic seams independent of whichever concrete UI/runtime happens to host them today. Next action — when host→JS event push lands for the streaming stage, define the event payload contract in ZeroCommon too (not in the WPF host), so any future .wasm subscriber inherits the same surface.
